text: When you write code, it is common to find mistakes, or bugs. The first step in debugging is identifying the bug. This means understanding what the program should do versus what it actually does. To solve the problem, you need a systematic approach. Instead of guessing, test each part of your code one by one to find the error. Useful debugging tools include debuggers, which let you pause and examine your code, and logging, where you add print statements to see the values of variables at different points. By following these steps, you can fix bugs more efficiently and improve your coding skills.
